title: KEEL prototype manifest
description: Manifest for a working UI/UX prototype ingested by /keel-refine. Captures only non-derivable intent: which file is the entry, the disposition (reference vs seed), whether the prototype's stack matches the target repo, and named screens/states the source cannot recover. Entry/route enumeration is autodetectable from the prototype itself and is not stored here (P4 — no redundant storage).

schema_version.description: Prototype manifest schema version. Bumps only on breaking shape changes.

entry.description: Relative path (from manifest location) to the entry HTML file. For multi-file prototypes this is typically `index.html`. For single-file prototypes the entry is the prototype itself.

mode.description: Disposition. `reference` (default): frontend-designer extracts visual/behavioral intent and rebuilds in the target stack; never copies markup or styles verbatim. `seed`: frontend-designer may import patterns/styles from the prototype as a starting point. Implementer never reads prototype source under either mode.

stack_match.description: True iff the prototype's framework/stack equals the target repo's framework/stack. Default false because the safe assumption is mismatch — frontend-designer must re-derive in the target stack. Setting true unlocks tighter pattern reuse in seed mode.

screens.description: Named screens or routes in the prototype. Used by backlog-drafter as decomposition cues — typically one F## per screen, with cross-cutting components extracted to their own F## when shared. Source cannot reliably recover screen names for hash-routed SPAs or single-file artifacts with multiple modal states; this field is the only place that signal lives.

label.description: Human-readable name for the screen (e.g. `Dashboard`, `Settings`, `Empty inbox`).

path.description: Optional URL path, hash route, or DOM selector identifying this screen within the prototype.

states.description: Named visual states for this screen (e.g. `empty`, `loaded`, `error`, `loading`). Mirrors Storybook CSF naming convention.

notes.description: Free-form prose for downstream agents. Use for caveats the manifest fields cannot express (e.g. `dropdown menus are placeholders, ignore for component design`).
